orchestrator.description: Coordinates project workflow. Delegates to specialized subagents like task-planner, ai-architect, backend-dev, frontend-dev, and others.

task-planner.description: Breaks large tasks into structured, step-by-step plans before implementation begins.

ai-architect.description: Designs AI system architecture and self-hosted AI stacks.

backend-dev.description: Handles backend services, database architecture, and backend performance optimization.

frontend-dev.description: Builds frontend applications and UI components.

rag-engineer.description: Manages RAG pipelines, optimization, and vector database tuning.

prompt-engineer.description: Designs prompts, guardrails, and LLM optimization strategies.

ml-engineer.description: Builds and evaluates machine learning pipelines.

data-engineer.description: Handles data pipelines, cleaning, and validation.

devops.description: Manages deployment, CI/CD pipelines, containerization, and infrastructure monitoring.

tester.description: Runs automated and manual tests, writes test cases and coverage reports.

reviewer.description: Reviews code for quality, security vulnerabilities, and best practices.

debugger.description: Debugs runtime issues, traces errors, and proposes fixes.

pr-generator.description: Generates pull request descriptions, changelogs, and commit summaries.

evaluator.description: Evaluates outputs for correctness, completeness, and quality.

hallucination-detector.description: Detects factual inconsistencies, hallucinations, and unsupported claims in generated content.

cost-optimizer.description: Analyzes and optimizes token usage, prompt efficiency, and inference costs.

github-publisher.description: Creates a new GitHub repository, generates a professional README and documentation, writes a structured commit message, and pushes the entire project. Invoke when the user wants to publish a local project to GitHub for the first time.
